questions.question: Explain the Union Find (Disjoint Set Union) data structure. List and briefly describe its two primary operations.
keyPoints: Union Find maintains a collection of disjoint sets
Find operation returns the representative/root of the set containing an element
Union operation merges two sets into one
Used in Kruskal's algorithm, cycle detection, and network connectivity problems

questions.question: Compare the time complexity of basic Union Find operations with and without path compression and union by rank optimizations.
keyPoints: Without optimizations: Find and Union operations take O(n) time
With path compression only: Amortized O(α(n)) ≈ O(1)
With union by rank only: O(log n) for both operations
With both optimizations: Amortized O(α(n)) inverse Ackermann function

questions.question: Implement the Union Find data structure in C++/Python with both path compression and union by rank optimizations. Explain your code and analyze the time complexity of each operation.
keyPoints: Code should include: parent array, rank/size array, find() with path compression, union() by rank
Path compression flattens the tree structure during Find operations
Union by rank attaches smaller tree under larger tree to maintain balance
Time complexity: Find - O(α(n)), Union - O(α(n)), making operations nearly constant

questions.question: Describe in detail how path compression and union by rank optimizations work in Union Find. Prove why their combination results in near-constant time amortized complexity.
keyPoints: Path compression: During Find, make each node point directly to root, flattening the tree
Union by rank: Track tree height/size, attach shallower tree under deeper tree
Inverse Ackermann function α(n) grows extremely slowly, practically constant ≤ 4 for all realistic n
Combined effect: Each operation takes O(α(n)) amortized time, making DSU practically O(1) per operation
